description: §52.15.5/§52.8 (S255 auto-make-safe) — an auth-scoped Tier-1 server-authority cell (`authority="server"` → always an UNSCOPED SELECT *) under <program auth="required"> fires I-SSR-AUTH-SCOPED-CLIENT-HYDRATED (info): the compiler AUTO-OMITS it from the anonymous-reachable SSR compose seed (no first-paint fill, no window.__scrml_ssr_state entry) rather than baking every user's rows into the first paint. It hydrates client-side behind its gated /__serverLoad fetch (401 for anon) — safe by construction. NO E-* (idiomatic code still compiles).
rationale: §52.15.5: a Tier-1 authority cell's SELECT * is unscoped; when it is auth-scoped (the app is auth-aware and the route is not explicitly public) its unscoped SSR pre-render would leak every row across users on the anonymous-reachable compose route. The compiler auto-makes-safe by omitting it from the seed + firing the I-SSR-AUTH-SCOPED-CLIENT-HYDRATED info lint (was W-SSR-PRERENDER-UNSCOPED warning pre-S255 — the tracking-warning-for-the-hole, retired once the hole was auto-closed). Row-scope it to @currentUser.id (§52.15.3) to restore first-paint acceleration for authorized viewers.
